name: Senegal

colors.symbolism: hope and religion; for Muslims, the colour of the Prophet, for Christians, the colour of hope, and for animists, fertility
wealth, knowledge, and the natural riches of the country
the blood shed in the struggle for independence and the determination to fight against underdevelopment

description: Three equal vertical stripes — green, yellow, and red — with a centred green five-pointed star on the yellow stripe.
history: Adopted on 20 August 1960 when Senegal seceded from the short-lived Mali Federation. The Federation flag was a vertical green-yellow-red tricolour bearing a black human figure (kanaga); when Senegal left, the kanaga was replaced by a green star. The Pan-African colour scheme was inherited from the flag of Ghana via the Mali Federation.

features.symbolism: the Pan-African colours, shared with Mali, Guinea, Cameroon, and other former French African colonies
the openness of Senegal to the five continents and the unity of the country; the green echoes the hope of the African renaissance
